review_method: Fresh isolated git worktree at /tmp/claude-2000/.../scratchpad/review-cluster-e (detached at 0a9dc23), NOT the fixer's worktree. Backend deps from the pre-existing shared /opt/abacus-python interpreter (fastapi/psycopg2/pydantic/pyjwt already present). Frontend deps via `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --offline` against the local pnpm content-store (no network fetch). All commands run against DATABASE_URL_TEST only, guarded by tests/conftest.py's ProdTruncationGuard.

approval_real_record.evidence: TailoringAgent.run (apps/api/app/agents/tailor_agent.py lines ~386-421 in the fix) now calls ApprovalRepository().create(user_id, 'application_submit', {kind: 'resume_tailor', resume_id, job_id, ...}) and returns approval_id/approval_status on TailorRunResult; the tailored Resume row is created approval_status='pending' (resume.py create()). ApprovalRepository._resolve -> _sync_resume (approval.py) flips the linked Resume.approvalStatus to approved/rejected atomically with the approval-status UPDATE, scoped by payload.kind == 'resume_tailor' so it never touches an application/email/offer approval. test_mv_resume_studio.py::TestTailorApprovalIsReal (4 tests: opens pending approval with real row in /approvals?status=all, version flips pending->approved on /approve, version flips to rejected on /reject, base resume stays 'approved' by default) all PASS with fresh DB evidence — the gate has real teeth, not decorative.

kind_scoped_no_coverletter_regression.evidence: apps/api/app/repositories/approval.py create(): the idempotent-refresh UPDATE now adds `AND "payload"->>'kind' IS NOT DISTINCT FROM %s` bound to payload.get('kind'). Confirmed cover_letters.py:404 and cover_letter_agent.py:1002 both already set kind='cover_letter' (pre-existing, untouched by this diff) and email_agent.py:412 sets kind='email' (also pre-existing/untouched) — so a resume_tailor approval (kind='resume_tailor') for the same job_id can never match/overwrite a pending cover_letter or email approval's row, and vice versa. test_mv_resume_studio.py::test_tailor_and_cover_letter_approvals_do_not_collide runs a tailor + a cover-letter approval for the SAME job and asserts BOTH remain separate pending cards (kinds present, tailor approval id unchanged, kind still 'resume_tailor') — PASS. Additionally ran the full pre-existing cover-letter + approvals regression suites (test_cover_letter_agent.py, test_cover_letter_studio.py, test_approvals.py, test_approval_modal.py = 41 tests) — all PASS, zero regressions.

noop_refund_correct.evidence: tailor_agent.py raises NoChangesApplied when result.changes==0 BEFORE any Resume.create or ApprovalRepository.create call (verified by reading the code: the raise sits above both). routers/agents.py::_execute_reserved_run's generic `except Exception` (it is not an HTTPException/QuotaExhaustedError/LLMUnavailableError) catches it, calls _refund_once() (refunds the reserved quota unconditionally), marks the AgentRun 'failed', then re-raises; run_tailor's own try/except NoChangesApplied converts it into an honest 200 body {resume_id: null, changes: 0, noChangesApplied: true, approvalRequired: false, message: '...not charged...'}. _pipeline_core wraps its own _dispatch('tailor',...) call in try/except NoChangesApplied and continues to the cover-letter step rather than failing the whole pipeline. Verified end-to-end with fresh evidence: test_mv_resume_studio.py::TestNoSilentBilledNoOp (5 tests) — honest result shape, no child Resume version created, no ApprovalRequest opened, UsageQuotaRepository shows runsUsed==0 after the no-op (net-zero — proves the refund actually happened, not just that billing was skipped), and the full /agents/pipeline/run tolerates the no-op and still reaches coverLetter + awaiting_approval. All PASS.

migration_additive.evidence: No new numbered migration file was added under apps/api/migrations/ (confirmed via `git show 0a9dc23:apps/api/migrations` — unchanged, last file is 0022_billing.sql). This project has no migration runner (ADR-TR-1, per the db.py docstring) — additive columns are added via lazy, idempotent DDL functions gated by a fast-path existence check + a transaction-scoped pg_advisory_xact_lock, exactly mirroring the pre-existing ensure_user_profile_columns/ensure_admin_user_columns pattern. The new ensure_resume_columns() (db.py) runs `ALTER TABLE "Resume" ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS "approvalStatus" text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'approved'` — additive only, no DROP/RENAME/ALTER TYPE/no destructive statement anywhere in the diff. Advisory lock id 7420240721 does not collide with the two pre-existing lock ids in the same file (7420240712, 7420240720). packages/db/src/schema.prisma was updated in parallel with a matching `approvalStatus String @default("approved")` field and an explanatory comment pointing at the lazy-DDL function — schema/DB stay in sync for documentation purposes even though Prisma is not the write path. Default 'approved' preserves every pre-existing resume version (base + historical tailored) as authoritative/downloadable — verified live by test_base_resume_stays_approved.

adapted_tests_preserve_intent.verdict: PASS — CRITICAL check cleared, no test weakened to hide a regression

per_file.change: Added a trivial _StubApprovals.create() stub to TailoringAgent's DI so this pre-existing pure-unit test (test_persisted_raw_text_reflects_tailored_bullets) stays DB-free now that TailoringAgent unconditionally depends on an ApprovalRepository.
per_file.reasoning: The pinned assertion (persisted raw_text reflects the TAILORED bullets, GAP-P6-TAIL-002) is byte-for-byte unchanged; only plumbing was added to satisfy the new constructor dependency. No assertion was removed or loosened.

per_file.change: test_tailoring_agent_wires_story_bank_into_evidence now wraps agent.run(...) in pytest.raises(NoChangesApplied) instead of a bare call.
per_file.reasoning: Read the stub service: _StubService.tailor returns TailorResult(bullets=originals, originals=originals) i.e. changes defaults to 0 (bullets==originals) — this run WOULD have hit the new NoChangesApplied path unconditionally, so the test would fail with an uncaught exception if left unwrapped; wrapping it in pytest.raises is the correct, honest adaptation, not a weakening. Critically, the pinned assertions (`assert 'Kubernetes' in captured['evidence_extra']` and `'Kafka' in ...`) are UNCHANGED and still execute, because captured['evidence_extra'] is populated inside the stub's tailor() method BEFORE the run() method's changes==0 check fires — i.e. the story-bank-evidence-folding behavior this test exists to pin is still fully exercised and still asserted.

per_file.change: test_tailor_run_accepts_explicit_resume_id now monkeypatches ResumeTailorService.tailor to force a genuine 1-bullet change instead of relying on the default LLM fixture.
per_file.reasoning: Comment explains the default fixture is tuned for the bundled base résumé and yields 0 accepted edits for the short BA variant, which would now honestly no-op (no resume_id) and make the test's real assertions inapplicable. The stub forces changes=1 so the run is genuine; the two assertions this test exists to pin — `child['parentId'] == created['id']` and `child['formatHash'] == created['formatHash']` (selected-résumé-is-the-parent wiring, not the base) — are UNCHANGED and still directly exercised against a real created child.

per_file.change: test_retailoring_corrupted_parent_stays_consistent now builds the 'corrupted' parent from the REAL base résumé content (the fixture tuned to rewrite it) plus one injected duplicate-evidenceRef row, instead of a synthetic 2-bullet resume.
per_file.reasoning: The synthetic 2-bullet resume would not produce genuine accepted rewrites against the record-replay LLM fixture and would now hit the no-op path, defeating the test. Switching to real base content (which the fixture demonstrably rewrites, per every other passing test in this file) while keeping the injected duplicate evidenceRef preserves the exact defect being pinned (duplicate-row dedup on re-tailor). The two load-bearing assertions — `len(refs) == len(set(refs))` (dedup) and `body['changes'] == len(diff['changes'])` (the original observed bug: run said 7, diff said 6) — are UNCHANGED.

adapted_tests_preserve_intent.overall: All 4 adaptations are necessitated by MV-resume-studio-003 itself (a 0-net-change run now honestly raises instead of silently creating a version) rather than by any attempt to dodge a real regression. In every case the ORIGINAL pinned assertion is still present and still executed against real behavior; only test SETUP/fixtures were adjusted so the scenario reaches a changes>0 state where the original assertions remain meaningful. Verified by reading full before/after diffs, not just the change summary in the commit message.

prohibited_patterns_scan_method: git diff 0a9dc23^ 0a9dc23 -- apps/api apps/web | grep -nE 'TODO|FIXME|as any|@ts-ignore|eslint-disable|Math\.random|--no-verify|except:\s*pass' — zero matches. Manual read of every changed production file (tailor_agent.py, db.py, approval.py, resume.py, agents.py, page.tsx, lib.ts, ApprovalModal.tsx, resumes.ts, schema.prisma) confirms no placeholder content, no suppressed errors (the one broad `except Exception` in _execute_reserved_run is pre-existing generic refund-on-any-failure plumbing, not newly introduced, and it re-raises after refunding — never swallows), no fabricated/random metrics (grounding_confidence is a deterministic token-overlap calculation over real bullet text, explicitly documented as non-random in its own docstring).
reasons: MV-resume-studio-001: approvalRequired is now backed by a real ApprovalRequest row with kind=resume_tailor; approve/reject flips the linked Resume.approvalStatus atomically. Verified with fresh DB evidence, not just code reading.

Kind-scoped idempotency correctly prevents a tailor approval and a cover-letter approval for the same job from colliding — the single highest-risk item in this review — proven both by a dedicated new test and by the full pre-existing cover-letter/approvals suite passing unchanged.

MV-resume-studio-003: 0-net-change runs are honest no-ops — no version, no approval, and the reserved quota is refunded (proven by runsUsed==0 after the no-op, not merely inferred from code reading). The pipeline tolerates the no-op without failing.

The Resume.approvalStatus column is added via the same lazy, idempotent, advisory-locked DDL pattern already established in this codebase (ADR-TR-1) — additive only, defaults existing rows to 'approved', no destructive DDL, no lock-id collision, Prisma schema kept in sync for documentation.

All 4 adapted tests were read in full (before vs after) and their original pinned assertions are intact and still meaningfully exercised — the adaptations only change test SETUP to avoid the new (correct) no-op path, not the assertions themselves.
Full regression sweep (115 backend + 428 frontend tests, tsc, next build) is green with zero failures and zero prohibited patterns found.
concerns: Minor, non-blocking: _execute_reserved_run's generic except-Exception path records the AgentRun as status='failed' with error=str(NoChangesApplied-message) for what is semantically an honest no-op rather than a true failure. This mirrors the pre-existing FabricationError handling for the cover-letter agent (same pattern, not introduced by this fix), so it's consistent with established precedent, but an admin/audit dashboard reading AgentRun.status='failed' could misread a no-op as an error. Not a correctness or security issue; suggest a follow-up ticket to add a distinct 'no_changes' terminal status if the admin UI ever surfaces raw AgentRun rows.

Minor, non-blocking: React 'not wrapped in act(...)' console warnings appear when running resume-mv-honesty.test.tsx and topbar.test.tsx under vitest. Tests still pass deterministically; this is pre-existing test-hygiene debt across the frontend suite, not something introduced or worsened by this diff.
